The series generated multi-decade highs and records across television viewership, social media engagement and merchandise sales.
In the first 24 hours following their clinching win in Game 5, the Knicks set an all-time Fanatics merchandise record for any championship team ever across all sports."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/nba-finals-2026-tv-ratings-best-since-1998-1236622090/","title":"NBA Finals TV Ratings Finish With Best Mark Since 1998","content":"The last time the finals drew 20 million-plus viewers over three consecutive contests was in 2016, for the final three games of the Cleveland Cavaliers’ comeback victory over the Golden State Warriors.
The telecast was also the most watched game five since the 1998 NBA Finals, which marked Michael Jordan’s last championship with the Chicago Bulls.
Saturday’s telecast peaked with a huge 33 million viewers who watched the closing minutes of the Knicks’ 94-90 win over the San Antonio Spurs.
The 2026 NBA Finals doubled last year’s average of 10.27 million viewers."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NBA_Finals_television_ratings","title":"NBA Finals television ratings - Wikipedia","content":"The highest rated and most watched NBA Finals series was the 1998 NBA Finals between the Chicago Bulls and Utah Jazz, which averaged an 18.7 rating / 33 share and 29.04 million viewers on NBC.
The 2019 NBA Finals between the Golden State Warriors and the Toronto Raptors had a drop in American viewership however combined North American viewership.
The average viewers figure over 6 games was 7.5 million, which is a 51% decline from the previous year."},{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/sports-business/article/2026-nba-finals-knicks-spurs-most-watched-finals-since-michael-jordan-capped-last-dance-with-bulls-in-1998-214810687.html","title":"2026 NBA Finals: Knicks-Spurs most-watched Finals since Michael Jordan capped 'Last Dance' with Bulls in 1998 - Yahoo Sports","content":"This year's Finals averaged 20.6 million viewers, a 100% increase in viewership from last year's championship series, which saw the Oklahoma City Thunder outlast the Indiana Pacers in seven games, per ESPN.
A series that featured a rematch of LeBron James' Cleveland Cavaliers against a dynastic Golden State Warriors squad averaged 20.41 million viewers, per Sports Media Watch, the year after the Cavs completed their unprecedented 3-1 comeback.
The 2026 NBA Finals set an ABC and ESPN audience record, according to ESPN, which cited Nielsen.
This year's version of Knicks-Spurs also secured the largest NBA Finals Game 5 audience since 1998, per ESPN.
In New York's closeout game at San Antonio's Frost Bank Center, Brunson — like Jordan in Game 6 of the '98 Finals — erupted for 45 points."},{"url":"https://www.sportico.com/business/media/2026/nba-finals-knicks-championship-tv-viewers-ratings-abc-1234909990/","title":"2026 NBA Finals Ratings Impress Even With Nielsen Measurement Context","content":"In earning their first NBA title in 53 years, the New York Knicks gifted the league and ABC the biggest TV turnout since Michael Jordan celebrated his final championship in 1998.
According to Nielsen Big Data + Panel estimates, the Knicks’ five-game defeat of the favored San Antonio Spurs averaged 20.6 million viewers per night, making this year’s installment of the Finals the most-watched since the Chicago Bulls beat the Utah Jazz in six games.
The aforementioned Bulls-Jazz set averaged an 18.7 household rating, with 33% of all TVs in use at the time of the live broadcasts tuned into NBC."}]}
